title: Linux Networking Tools
description: Explore modern and classic networking utilities including ip, ss, netstat, curl, dig, and traceroute for connectivity diagnostics.

tasks.prompt: Display all network interface addresses and link states.
tasks.hint: Use ip addr show: 'ip addr show'
tasks.explanation: 'ip addr show' replaces 'ifconfig' on modern Linux, listing IP addresses assigned to each interface.

tasks.prompt: Bring the network interface 'eth0' up (administratively enable it).
tasks.hint: Use ip link set: 'sudo ip link set eth0 up'
tasks.explanation: 'ip link set eth0 up' enables the interface so it can send and receive traffic.

tasks.hint: Use ip route show: 'ip route show'
tasks.explanation: 'ip route show' lists all routes the kernel uses to forward packets to destinations.

tasks.prompt: List all TCP listening sockets with process information.
tasks.explanation: 'ss -tlnp' shows TCP (-t) listening (-l) numeric (-n) sockets with owning processes (-p).

tasks.prompt: List all listening TCP ports numerically using netstat.
tasks.explanation: 'netstat -tln' is the classic tool for viewing listening TCP ports; 'ss' is preferred on newer systems.

tasks.prompt: Fetch only the HTTP response headers from 'https://example.com'.
tasks.hint: Use curl with head flag (-I): 'curl -I https://example.com'
tasks.explanation: 'curl -I' sends a HEAD request and prints response headers without the body.

tasks.hint: Use curl with output flag (-o): 'curl -o app.tar.gz https://releases.example.com/app.tar.gz'
tasks.explanation: 'curl -o' writes the downloaded content to the specified local filename.

tasks.prompt: Query DNS MX records for the domain 'example.com'.
tasks.hint: Use dig with record type: 'dig example.com MX'
tasks.explanation: 'dig' performs DNS lookups. Specifying 'MX' queries mail exchange records for the domain.

tasks.prompt: Resolve the A record for 'google.com' and show only the answer (short output).
tasks.hint: Use dig with short flag: 'dig +short google.com'
tasks.explanation: 'dig +short' returns just the resolved IP addresses without verbose diagnostic output.

tasks.prompt: Trace the network route (hops) to host '8.8.8.8'.
tasks.explanation: 'traceroute' shows each router hop between your host and the destination, useful for path diagnosis.

tasks.prompt: Display the fully qualified domain name (FQDN) of this host.
tasks.explanation: 'hostname -f' prints the complete DNS name including domain suffix of the machine.

tasks.prompt: Send exactly 3 ICMP echo requests to '1.1.1.1'.
tasks.explanation: 'ping -c 3' limits the probe to three packets, providing a quick reachability test.
